title: Sulcorebutia lanata
taxonomy: Sulcorebutia lanata is a species within the cactus family, Cactaceae. It is classified as a type of flowering plant, belonging to the order Caryophyllales. Specifically, it falls under the family Cactaceae, subfamily Cactoideae, and tribe Rebutieae. Sulcorebutia lanata further belongs to the genus Sulcorebutia, which contains around 20 species of small to medium-sized cacti. The classification is part of the broader system of plant taxonomy.
uses: Sulcorebutia lanata is a species of cactus that has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in South America. Its medicinal uses include treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's extracts have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate pain and reduce swelling. In traditional medicine, Sulcorebutia lanata is also used to treat respiratory issues and as a natural diuretic. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy of these uses and ensure their safety for human consumption.

intro: Sulcorebutia lanata, also known as woolly Sulcorebutia, is a species of cactus in the genus Sulcorebutia, characterized by its woolly or hairy stems and flowers. This plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate skin conditions and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Sulcorebutia lanata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Sulcorebutia lanata is often used in traditional medicine and is commonly prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and topical creams to harness its potential health benefits.
